title: Two ifs both run
problem: Grade a score of 85: 'distinction' for 80 and up, otherwise 'pass' for 50 and up.

trace.note: First if: 85 >= 80, so grade = 'distinction'. So far so good.
A second, separate `if` — it runs too, because it isn't joined to the first. And 85 >= 50 is also true.
trace.checkpoint_prompt: grade is already 'distinction'. The next line is a separate `if score >= 50:`. After it runs, what will grade be?

trace.note: grade = 'pass', overwriting 'distinction'. Both ifs fired and the second clobbered the first. We wanted only one branch to win.
